betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Alloy Furnace: Smelting Alloys with Power§f

§aThe Alloy Furnace produces NC's alloy ingots at scale using FE.§f
The §bAlloy Furnace§f (`nuclearcraft:alloy_furnace`) is NuclearCraft's §bmulti-input alloy smelter§f. It accepts §bup to 4 input items§f and §b1 fluid input§f, smelts them into §bNC alloy ingots§f and §bchance-based secondary outputs§f, and operates on §bForge Energy (FE)§f.

§6Crafting Recipe (graph: minecraft.crafting)§f:
Per the JEI graph, the Alloy Furnace recipe requires:
- §bRedstone§f (`minecraft:redstone`)
- §bBrick§f (`minecraft:brick`)
- §bFurnace§f (`minecraft:furnace`)
- §bCopper Solenoid§f (`nuclearcraft:part:4`)

§6Function§f:
- §bProcess: alloy smelting§f — smelts §b§f§cNC alloy recipes§f§f (e.g. §bTough Alloy§f, §bHard Carbon Alloy§f, §b§f§cBoron§f§f alloys, §b§f§cMagnesium§f§f alloys).
- §bSlots: 4 item inputs + 1 fluid input§f → §b1 item output + 1 fluid output§f.
- §bPower: FE only§f — like the Nuclear Furnace, no fuel slot.
- §bUpgrades: Speed / Energy§f — both upgrades can be inserted into the UI.

§6What the Alloy Furnace Produces§f:
- §bTough Alloy Ingot§f (the most common early-game alloy)
- §bHard Carbon Alloy Ingot§f (mid-game, used in §bOre Leacher§f and high-tier machines)
- §b§f§cBeryllium Alloy Ingot§f§f, §b§f§cBoron Carbide Ingot§f§f, §b§f§cTough Alloy§f§f variants
- §bMelodic Alloy Ingot§f (§bNC Alloy Furnace§f uses §bNether Star§f + §bStellar Alloy Ingot§f)
- Many more (see `nc/recipe/processor/AlloyFurnaceRecipes.java`)

§6Per-Recipe Examples (AlloyFurnaceRecipes.java)§f:
- §bTough Alloy§f: Iron + Coal + Limestone flux → Tough Alloy Ingot (early-game)
- §bMelodic Alloy§f: §bNether Star§f + §bStellar Alloy Ingot§f × 2 → Melodic Alloy Ingot (line 80, late-game)

§6Upgrade Slots§f:
- §bSpeed Upgrade§f (`nuclearcraft:upgrade:0`)
- §bEnergy Upgrade§f (`nuclearcraft:upgrade:1`)

§6Component Supply Chain§f:
- §bCopper Solenoid§f → from §bCopper Ingot§f + §bRedstone§f + §bIron Ingot§f in a vanilla crafting grid (per §bCraftingRecipeHandler.java§f)
- §bFurnace§f → 8 × Cobblestone
- §bBrick§f → 4 × §bBrick Block§f in a vanilla crafting grid (or §b§f§cfurnace output§f§f)
- §bRedstone§f → from §bRedstone Ore§f mining

§eTip§f: Set up a §btriple-Alloy Furnace line§f in your base — one Alloy Furnace for §bTough Alloy§f, one for §bHard Carbon Alloy§f, and one for §bendgame alloys§f. Each line should have at least 4 §bSpeed Upgrades§f to maximize throughput.
